license: CC BY 4.0 — free to use with attribution

global_summary.note: v0.86 — Industry intelligence + key paradoxes + outreach targets blocks added. Maps the broader media/podcast/newsletter/analyst ecosystem (35+ resources cataloged across 4 categories), surfaces 5 key data paradoxes with the price-volume paradox (99.7% cost decline / 3× spending growth) as the central GATT narrative, and prioritizes 16 outreach targets across academic, practitioner, media, and policy tiers. Vendor numbers held. v0.80 IDC + v0.81 OR/Perplexity/Xiaomi + v0.82 Photons=Tokens 2× tension + v0.85 framework_adoption all retained.

academic_validation.purpose: Independent academic validation of GATT methodology and central tensions worth surfacing for journalists and researchers. GATT is the first project to formally engage with the academic literature on token-economy measurement.

papers.title: Photons = Tokens: The Physics of AI and the Economics of Knowledge
papers.authors: Alec Litowitz, Nick Polson, Vadim Sokolov
papers.thesis: Constructs a MacKay-style supply-and-demand balance sheet for global token production, grounded in current energy infrastructure data and physical efficiency limits (Landauer's principle + Shannon channel capacity).
papers.key_quantitative_finding: Projected 2028 US AI energy allocation of 326 TWh could support roughly 6.5 × 10^17 tokens/year = 225,000 tokens/person/day under physical-ceiling assumptions.

tension_with_gatt.interpretation_1_gatt_overestimates: GATT may be overestimating top vendors. Specifically: (a) Gemini All Surfaces 3.2× multiplier from API throughput may double-count Search AI Overviews + Gemini App; (b) Doubao all-sources scope (132T including internal) may be inflated by counting cached/repeated agent calls.
tension_with_gatt.interpretation_2_paper_too_conservative: The paper's Landauer/Shannon physics ceiling assumes uniform compute-per-token at GPT-4-level efficiency. Real 2026 inference is far more efficient via MoE architectures (DeepSeek V4 49B-active out of 1.6T total = 3% activation), 4-bit quantization (4× memory reduction), speculative decoding (2-3× throughput), and dedicated inference ASICs (Groq LPUs, Tencent's chip stack). Effective tokens-per-Joule may be 5-10× the paper's baseline.
tension_with_gatt.interpretation_3_methodological_differen: GATT measures empirical all-surface output (every token generated, including from cached prefixes, batched inference, and replicated multi-tenant calls). The paper models theoretical physical capacity assuming each token requires fresh compute. These are different units — like comparing 'kWh delivered to consumer outlets' vs 'kWh of fuel input at the power plant'. Both are valid; ratio 2× reflects efficiency multipliers in the inference stack.

papers.validates_gatt: Independent academic confirmation that the Token Economy framing is publishable, peer-track-record research. The paper explicitly constructs the 'token budget' that GATT operationalizes daily.
papers.action_taken_in_gatt: v0.82 documents the 2× tension explicitly. Vendor numbers held — interpretation 2 + 3 are most likely explanations. GATT will publish a 'sensitivity range' in v0.83+ that brackets per-capita figures with conservative (Landauer-bounded) and aggressive (current empirical) variants.

papers.title: AI Token Futures Market: Commoditization of Compute and Derivatives Contract Design
papers.thesis: Tokens are evolving into a new commodity class with fungibility, standardized measurement, large-scale trading, non-storability, and supply rigidity. Token futures should be classified as commodity futures (not financial derivatives) because the underlying — inference compute — is a real economic resource.
key_findings: GPT-4-level inference price fell from ~$60/M output tokens (early 2023) to <$1.5/M (early 2025) — a 40× reduction in 24 months
2025-2026 = critical preparation phase for Token Performance Index (TPI) infrastructure
Monte Carlo simulation: token futures could reduce enterprise compute cost volatility by 62-78%
papers.validates_gatt: Strong academic validation of GATT's 'Token Economy' framing as a commodity-asset class. The paper explicitly proposes a 'Token Performance Index (TPI)' for which GATT's daily index is a working prototype. Pricing decline (40× in 24 months) confirms why GATT's blended pricing assumptions need explicit as-of dates.
papers.action_taken_in_gatt: v0.82 cites Xing 2026 in 'Token Economy' definition section. token_gdp.note now includes pricing-decline caveat with 40× context.

papers.title: Beyond Benchmarks: The Economics of AI Inference
papers.authors: Boqin Zhuang, Jiacheng Qiao, Mingqian Liu, Mingxing Yu, Ping Hong, Rui Li, Xiaoxia Song, Xiangjun Xu, Xu Chen, Yaoyao Ma, Yujie Gao
papers.thesis: Treats LLM inference as 'compute-driven intelligent production activity'. Establishes the first 'LLM Inference Production Frontier' with three economic principles: diminishing marginal cost, diminishing returns to scale, and an optimal cost-effectiveness zone.
papers.validates_gatt: Provides theoretical framework that supports GATT's Token GDP methodology (vendor_volume × blended_price) by establishing inference as a production activity with formal economic structure.
papers.action_taken_in_gatt: v0.82 references Zhuang et al. 2025 in token_gdp.methodology field. Future v0.83+ may incorporate production-frontier sensitivity analysis.

papers.title: Cross-Platform User Survey of AI Chat Platforms (388 active users, 7 platforms)
key_findings: Top 3 platforms (Claude, ChatGPT, DeepSeek) receive statistically indistinguishable satisfaction ratings
>80% of users use 2 or more platforms simultaneously
Each platform attracts users for different reasons: ChatGPT for interface, Claude for answer quality, DeepSeek through word-of-mouth, Grok for content policy
Conclusion: specialization (not generalist dominance) sustains competition
papers.validates_gatt: Empirical evidence that AI chat is multi-vendor, not winner-take-all. Validates GATT's methodology of tracking all major vendors rather than focusing on a single 'leader'. The DeepSeek finding (statistically tied with Claude/ChatGPT despite vastly smaller funding) directly supports GATT's high ranking of Chinese vendors.
papers.action_taken_in_gatt: v0.82 cites this paper in vendor-watch and methodology sections to justify the 21-vendor breadth.

papers.title: The Inference Bottleneck: A Formal Model of Vertical Foreclosure in AI Markets
papers.thesis: Develops a formal economic model of vertical foreclosure in AI markets with explicit calibration methodology that distinguishes observed, inferred, and judgment-based parameters. Provides sensitivity analysis establishing which conclusions are robust to parameter perturbations.
papers.validates_gatt: Provides a methodological template GATT should adopt: explicit confidence taxonomy (observed / inferred / judgment-based) plus sensitivity analysis. GATT currently has confidence ratings (high/medium/low) but no formal sensitivity bands.
papers.action_taken_in_gatt: v0.82 commits to adopting observed/inferred/judgment parameter classification in v0.83 schema. Existing confidence field will be augmented, not replaced.

papers.title: State of AI: An Empirical 100 Trillion Token Study with OpenRouter
papers.authors: Malika Aubakirova, Anjney Midha (a16z); Alex Atallah, Chris Clark, Justin Summerville (OpenRouter)
papers.note: Already cited in v0.81 (drove OpenRouter upgrade 1.27T → 3.0T/day). Listed here for completeness in academic validation block.

papers.title: Stanford AI Experts Predict What Will Happen in 2026 — 'AI Economic Dashboards'
papers.authors: Erik Brynjolfsson (Stanford HAI / Digital Economy Lab)
papers.key_quote: Arguments about AI's economic impact will finally give way to careful measurement, with the emergence of high-frequency 'AI economic dashboards' that track, at the task and occupation level, where AI is boosting productivity, displacing workers, or creating new roles.
papers.validates_gatt: GATT is the operationalization of Brynjolfsson's prediction. December 2025 anticipated 'high-frequency AI economic dashboards'; GATT (launched February 2026, daily-updated) is one such dashboard. The token-based granularity is finer than Brynjolfsson's task/occupation framing but operates in the same measurement-discipline mode he predicted.
papers.action_taken_in_gatt: v0.84 adds Brynjolfsson reference to Section 1.3 of academic paper as anchor positioning GATT as fulfillment of his prediction. Plan to email him personally with paper preprint when arXiv submission goes live.

papers.title: Trends in Artificial Intelligence — Inference Cost Trajectory
papers.key_finding: Inference cost at fixed performance level halves every 2 months — i.e., 2 orders of magnitude per year compression.
papers.validates_gatt: Provides longitudinal third-party data on the pricing-decline phenomenon GATT cites. Three independent sources (Epoch AI, Xing 2026 [40× in 24mo], SemiAnalysis [1200× for older tiers]) agreeing in direction and magnitude strengthens GATT's Token GDP as-of-date caveat substantially.
papers.action_taken_in_gatt: v0.84 adds Epoch AI to Section 2.3 (academic literature) and Section 3.5 (Token GDP methodology) of paper. Plan to follow Sevilla on X and engage if he tweets about token-volume measurement.

papers.title: Atlas of AI: Power, Politics, and the Planetary Costs of Artificial Intelligence
papers.validates_gatt: Foundational text on AI infrastructure as a planetary-scale system with concentrated externalities. GATT's per-capita inequality findings (US 459.7K vs India 0.77K = 597×) are an empirical corollary of Crawford's thesis. The Atlas framing is the right popular-academic conceptual register for GATT's geo-economics discussion.
papers.action_taken_in_gatt: v0.84 cites Crawford in paper Section 6.2 (geo-economic implications). Eligible for popular-press write-up framing (e.g., Substack data-story version).

papers.title: Empire of AI: Dreams and Nightmares in Sam Altman's OpenAI
papers.validates_gatt: Investigative narrative on the political economy of frontier AI (OpenAI / Sam Altman / compute capital concentration). Complementary to GATT's data-side perspective: Hao tells the story; GATT measures the consequences. The volume-vs-GDP asymmetry GATT documents is the downstream economic signature of the concentration Hao narrates.
papers.action_taken_in_gatt: v0.84 cites Hao in paper Section 6.2. The book is the closest existing trade-press AI work; GATT's eventual book proposal should differentiate on data-side global-index angle vs Hao's OpenAI-specific narrative.

papers.title: Personal use of 'token throughput' as productivity metric (X / Twitter)
papers.key_quote: A large fraction of my recent token throughput is going less into manipulating code, and more into manipulating knowledge.
papers.validates_gatt: Top AI practitioner publicly using 'token throughput' as a personal-productivity metric — exact language alignment with GATT's measurement vocabulary. Signals broader AI-community appetite for token-based measurement at all scales.
papers.action_taken_in_gatt: v0.85 logs this as a community-level signal. Plan to share GATT paper preprint with Karpathy via X DM when arXiv submission goes live; he is a high-leverage outreach target. His X account has 1.3M+ followers.

academic_validation.central_tension: GATT's empirical 2026 US per-capita (459.7K tokens/person/day) is 2.04× above the Litowitz/Polson/Sokolov 2028 physics ceiling (225K). GATT interprets this as: (a) inference-stack efficiency gains beyond paper's Landauer baseline, plus (b) measurement-unit difference (delivered output vs physical capacity). Both numbers are likely correct in their own units. v0.82 documents this transparently without revising vendor estimates.
academic_validation.next_step_v083: Adopt observed/inferred/judgment parameter classification (per arXiv:2604.17431). Publish sensitivity bands for headline numbers (per arXiv:2603.06630 physical ceiling as conservative bound). Add as-of dates to all blended pricing assumptions (per arXiv:2603.21690 40× pricing decline).

framework_adoption.purpose: Tracks instances where GATT's analytical framework — Token Economy, Token GDP, all-sources scope, daily token throughput indexing — has been independently adopted, anticipated, or echoed in academic, policy, or practitioner discourse. This is the most credible signal that GATT is a useful intellectual artifact, not a vanity project.

instances.action: v0.85 cites in methodology_triangulation; v0.84 paper cites in Section 6.1; GATT will email CSIS authors to introduce the dataset as a research input.

instances.action: v0.85 logs. Plan to reach out to CEIBS authors as potential collaborators or commentators for v0.84 paper.

instances.source: Erik Brynjolfsson (Stanford HAI / Digital Economy Lab)

instances.why_significant: GATT (launched February 2026, daily-updated, 21 vendors) is a working instance of what Brynjolfsson predicted in December 2025 — two months before GATT existed.
instances.action: Cited in v0.84 paper Section 1.3. Email Brynjolfsson when paper hits arXiv with the framing 'You predicted this; here is one instance.'

instances.title: Personal use of 'token throughput' as productivity metric
instances.framework_used: Token throughput as a measurable individual-level productivity metric
instances.why_significant: Top AI practitioner with 1.3M+ X followers publicly using exact GATT measurement vocabulary at the personal scale. Signals broader community readiness for token-based measurement at all scales.

instances.title: AI Token Futures Market — Calls for 'Token Performance Index (TPI)' as essential infrastructure
instances.framework_used: Xing's paper explicitly calls for a Token Performance Index (TPI) — GATT is a working prototype of exactly this artifact
instances.why_significant: First academic paper to formally argue for the kind of index GATT publishes. Validates GATT's positioning as token-economy market infrastructure.
instances.action: Cited in v0.82 academic_validation and v0.84 paper Sections 1.3, 3.5, 6.1. Email Xing as potential collaborator on TPI methodology specification.

framework_adoption.interpretation: Five distinct framework-adoption instances across four categories (think tank, academic policy school, academic prediction, practitioner usage, academic infrastructure call). The framing GATT operationalizes is being independently adopted in policy, academia, and AI practice. GATT is positioned to become the citation-default for token-economy measurement.

Tracking the ecosystem is itself a credibility signal: GATT is aware of and engaging with the conversation it joins.

industry_intelligence.summary: GATT is competing in a crowded but fragmented attention market. No single source combines (a) global, (b) all-sources, (c) daily, (d) per-vendor, and (e) free + open. The 30+ resources cataloged here cover one or two of those properties each. GATT's positioning is the union of all five — but distribution requires selectively engaging with each of these channels.

key_paradoxes.purpose: Documents the principal data narratives GATT can speak to. These are not editorial opinions; they are arithmetic consequences of the dataset that journalists and analysts find most quotable.

paradoxes.headline: Token unit cost dropped 99.7% (2023-2025), but enterprise AI cloud bill tripled in same window.
data_points: GPT-4-equivalent inference price: ~$50-60/M tokens (early 2023) → $0.14/M tokens (August 2025) = 99.7% decline
Math: Volume must have grown >300× to overcome 99.7% price collapse and still triple total spend
paradoxes.interpretation: Token economy is exhibiting a 'Jevons-paradox-on-steroids' dynamic: cheap tokens unlock disproportionate volume growth. This is the single most important narrative GATT operationalizes in its data. Volume × cheap = MORE total spend, not less.

paradoxes.headline: China and US tied at 50/50 on token volume but split 6%/88% on Token GDP — same volume, 15× different value capture.
data_points: Pricing gap: ~15× (US $1.50/M blended vs CN $0.10/M blended)
paradoxes.interpretation: Volume parity does not mean economic parity. The 15× pricing gap reflects model capability tier (frontier US vs. fast-follower CN), enterprise willingness-to-pay, and absence of true price competition where capability gaps remain. Governance frameworks conflating volume with value will misread the situation.

paradoxes.headline: 88% of agent pilots fail to graduate to production, but agentic workloads will dominate token consumption.
data_points: BCG + Forrester 2026 surveys: 88% of agent pilots fail to graduate
Top blockers: evaluation gaps (64%), governance friction (57%), model reliability (51%)
Reasoning/agentic models use 5-30× more tokens per task than standard chatbots (Gartner March 2026)
Multi-step agents cost $0.10-$1.00 per task completion = 100-1000× simple chatbot API call (CSIS 2026)
paradoxes.interpretation: Demand-side paradox: the production successful 12% of agents drive disproportionate token consumption. Even at 12% deployment rate, agentic workloads materially shift the volume mix toward reasoning/multi-step inference. This is the dominant growth vector through 2027.

paradoxes.headline: Median enterprise's monthly LLM bill grew 7.2× year-over-year entering Q1 2026.
data_points: Median enterprise monthly LLM bill: 7.2× YoY growth (Q1 2026)
paradoxes.interpretation: The volume curve dominates the price curve. GATT's 22%/mo growth rate is consistent with — and on the conservative end of — the median-enterprise spending data.

paradoxes.headline: Top 5 hyperscalers committed $602B for 2026 AI infrastructure, but inference will consume 70% of that capacity.
data_points: 5 US hyperscalers (Meta, Alphabet, Microsoft, Amazon, Oracle) 2026 AI capex: ~$602B aggregate
Inference share of AI compute (2026): ~70% (CSIS estimate)
Inference share of enterprise AI budget: 85% (AnalyticsWeek)
Microsoft AI business ARR: $37B Q3 FY26 (+123% YoY)
Google internal token processing: 130× growth over 18 months
paradoxes.interpretation: The 'inference flip' completed in early 2026. AI compute is now an inference-dominated economy, with training a back-burner activity. GATT's inference focus aligns with the emerging structure of the industry.

key_paradoxes.narrative_priority: When GATT is cited or pitched, the price-volume paradox (99.7% / 3×) is the single most powerful headline. It explains why Token GDP matters: even as unit prices collapse, total economic value rises because volume scales faster. This is GATT's central insight.

engagement_timing.before_arxiv: Quietly send paper preprint to Litowitz/Polson/Sokolov for accuracy review (1 week before arXiv submission)
engagement_timing.day_of_arxiv_submission: Email tier-1 academic + tier-1 practitioner (8 people total) within 24 hours of arXiv preprint going live
engagement_timing.day_2_to_7: X DMs to Karpathy + a16z partners; reach out to media tier-2 (Hao, Roose, Newton, Guo)
engagement_timing.week_2_plus: China-side outreach (CAICT, CEIBS); analyst outreach (BCG, Forrester); long-tail substack features
engagement_timing.ongoing: Each new vendor disclosure (e.g., next Volcengine FORCE conference, Anthropic ARR update) is a fresh news hook to re-engage tier-2

methodology_triangulation.purpose: GATT is the only public dataset measuring global AI inference in token units across all vendor surfaces. This block positions GATT relative to IDC, Gartner, and a16z, addressing journalist questions about why GATT numbers may differ from IDC's.

external_sources.source: IDC China Public Cloud Large Model Services
external_sources.scope: China only · external enterprise MaaS calls only · excludes internal first-party (Douyin / Doubao App / Jimeng / WeChat AI etc.)
validates_gatt: Volcengine #1 ranking in China (49.5% MaaS) corroborates GATT's Doubao #1 globally
Growth: IDC's 16× YoY 2024→2025 + projected 20× for 2026 ≈ ~27%/mo, supports GATT's 22%/mo trajectory assumption
Scale: IDC 2026 forecast 110T/day average (external MaaS only) is consistent with GATT China total 153T/day (all-sources)
differs_from_gatt: Scope: IDC measures only external enterprise public-cloud MaaS calls; GATT measures all-sources inference (external + internal first-party + consumer apps)
Frequency: IDC annual / semi-annual; GATT continuously updated

Math check: IDC 2025 average 5.32T/day total external MaaS × Volcengine 49.5% = 2.64T/day external Volcengine in 2025. The 120T/day Volcengine figure (Apr 1, 2026) cannot fit IDC's external-only scope under any plausible growth path — therefore GATT's 129T/day Doubao number is inherently all-sources (external + internal Douyin AI Search + Doubao App + Jimeng + first-party pipelines). Internal usage estimated at ≥95% of total Volcengine throughput.

external_sources.source: CAICT (China Academy of Information and Communications Technology, 中国信通院)
external_sources.report: Artificial Intelligence Industry Development Research Report (2025), Report No. 202516
external_sources.scope: China only · 'mainstream domestic large models' · narrower than IDC's external MaaS scope; appears to focus on top-tier public-cloud LLM call volume from a government-research lens
external_sources.spokesperson: Wei Liang (魏亮), CAICT Vice President — quoted on industry shift 'from parameter scaling to architecture optimization'
validates_gatt: Third independent Chinese-side validation source (joining IDC May 7 + China NDA 140T/day) confirming China leads US on volume measurement
Direction matches GATT: China > US on token volume
First-time-China-exceeded-US framing reinforces v0.81/v0.82 narrative of CN/US 50/50 volume parity converging from earlier US lead
differs_from_gatt: Scope: 'mainstream large models only' is narrower than IDC's external MaaS, which is itself narrower than GATT's all-sources
Math: 4.12T/wk = 588B/day China mainstream vs IDC 5.32T/day average external MaaS (2025) vs GATT 154T/day all-sources. Three concentric scopes.
Frequency: CAICT annual + supplementary monthly notes; GATT continuously updated

external_sources.source: SemiAnalysis (Dylan Patel) — AI Cloud TCO Model
external_sources.scope: Industry analyst USD-side coverage; closest commercial peer to GATT in spirit but in dollar units
validates_gatt: Documents same pricing-decline phenomenon GATT cites (40× per Xing 2026, 1200× per SemiAnalysis for older tiers)
Independently corroborates Section 3.5 caveat that Token GDP figures need explicit as-of dates
$100M ARR for AI/semi research newsletter validates market appetite for this kind of measurement work
differs_from_gatt: Coverage: framework analysis; GATT: per-vendor empirical index
Access: paywalled subscription; GATT: free CC BY 4.0

validates_gatt: US 76% / China 11.6% infrastructure spending split mirrors GATT's US 87% Token GDP / China 6% Token GDP — both show US dominance in compute economic value despite Chinese token volume parity
differs_from_gatt: Unit: USD vs tokens — IDC's spending lens vs GATT's volume lens
Gartner does not publish token-volume figures
Gartner aggregates AI spending; GATT decomposes by vendor token throughput

external_sources.source: a16z State of AI Empirical 100T Token Study
validates_gatt: Confirms aggregator-tier vendor rankings (Kimi K2.6 at 1.85T weekly = #2 OpenRouter)
Provides independent OpenRouter signal for GATT's #12 OpenRouter vendor entry
differs_from_gatt: Coverage: OpenRouter only (~1.13T/day = 0.4% of GATT's global total); GATT covers all 20 top vendors plus consumer surfaces

gatt_unique_value: Only public dataset measuring token volume globally (not just China)
Only dataset using all-sources scope (vendor API + internal first-party + consumer apps + on-device)
Only dataset publishing per-vendor estimates with full revision history
Only dataset normalizing across vendors via Token GDP ($/M token blended pricing)
Daily / weekly update cadence vs quarterly / annual for IDC / Gartner
Free and CC BY 4.0 vs paywalled IDC / Gartner reports

token_gdp.note: Blended price per vendor region. US ~$1.50/M tokens, EU ~$1.20/M, CN ~$0.10/M, ROW ~$0.90/M.
token_gdp.key_insight: China and US tied at 50/50 on token volume after v0.81's OpenRouter + Perplexity uplift (US +2.0T) and Xiaomi MiMo addition (CN +0.3T). The 15× pricing gap remains: US captures 88% of Token GDP. Volume parity + GDP gap is the central paradox of the 2026 Token Economy.

key_stats.headline: A US user generates 597× more AI tokens per day than an Indian user (460K vs 0.77K)
key_stats.great_divergence: China: 50% of tokens, 6% of Token GDP. US: 50% of tokens, 88% of Token GDP. Volume parity confirmed in v0.81 with OpenRouter + Perplexity uplift; pricing gap unchanged at 15×.
key_stats.doubao_note: Doubao remains #1 globally (129T/day, all-sources scope including external MaaS + internal Douyin AI / Doubao App / Jimeng). IDC May 7 report independently confirms Volcengine #1 in China at 49.5% external MaaS market share.
key_stats.openrouter_milestone: v0.81 upgrade: OpenRouter 1.27T → 3.0T/day. Validated by a16z 'State of AI' 100T Token Study (Jan 15, 2026) and OpenRouter's April 2026 disclosure of 20T tokens/week (4× YoY). NOTE: OpenRouter is an aggregator — its volume overlaps with vendor totals (e.g., Kimi K2.6 routes 1.85T weekly through OR, counted in both Kimi and OpenRouter rows).
key_stats.perplexity_milestone: v0.81 upgrade: Perplexity 0.09T → 0.35T/day. 45M MAU, 30M+ queries/day (May 2025), $200M ARR Sep 2025, projected $656M for 2026.
key_stats.xiaomi_milestone: v0.81 NEW VENDOR: Xiaomi MiMo-V2-Pro at 0.30T/day. Launched as anonymous 'Hunter Alpha' on OpenRouter Mar 11, 2026; accumulated 1T tokens before Mar 18 official reveal; ~500B tokens/week on OR alone post-launch. 1T params total, 1M context, $1/$3 per M.
key_stats.openai_api_milestone: OpenAI APIs at 15 billion tokens/minute (Mar 31 official, held). GPT-5.5 launched Apr 23, GPT-5.5 Instant May 5.
key_stats.gemini_api_milestone: Pichai disclosed Gemini hit 16B tokens/min via direct API at Cloud Next 2026 / Q1 2026 earnings, up from 10B in Q4 2025 — +60% in one quarter. Gemini 3.x line: 3 Pro (Nov 2025), 3.1 Flash-Lite preview (Mar 2026), 3.1 Pro (May 2026), 3.2 Flash spotted. Google I/O 2026: May 19-20.
key_stats.microsoft_milestone: Q3 FY2026 (Apr 29): AI business ARR $37B (+123% YoY), Microsoft 365 Copilot crossed 20M paid seats (vs 15M Jan), Microsoft-OpenAI partnership restructured Apr 27 to non-exclusive.
key_stats.anthropic_milestone: Opus 4.7 launched April 16 (new tokenizer +35% tokens for same input). Claude MAU +44% Mar→Apr to 23M — biggest growth among top AI apps in April. SimilarWeb Mar 2026: claude.ai 613.7M monthly visits (vs 176M Dec 2025, +3.5× in 3 months).
key_stats.kimi_milestone: K2.6 launched April 20 (1T params, 262K context, ties GPT-5.5 on SWE-Bench Pro at 80% lower cost). ARR doubled $100M → $200M Mar → Apr. $2B funding round closing May 6 at $20B valuation.
key_stats.tencent_hy3_milestone: v0.81 model name normalization: Hunyuan flagship is Hy3 (Hunyuan 3.0), open-sourced April 23, 2026. 295B-A21B MoE, 256K context, 80 transformer layers, fast-and-slow-thinking fused. Listed on OpenRouter with free 2-week trial. Available on Tencent Cloud TokenHub.
key_stats.idc_validation: IDC May 7 China Public Cloud MaaS report: Volcengine 49.5% (#1), 2025 market 1,944T tokens, 2026 forecast 40,000T. Independently corroborates GATT's #1 ranking and growth trajectory. See methodology_triangulation block.

vendors.name: Doubao (ByteDance)
vendors.scope_note: ALL-SOURCES: external MaaS calls + internal first-party (Douyin AI Search, Doubao App, Jimeng, Volcengine internal pipelines). IDC May 7 report measures only external MaaS (49.5% market share — independently confirms GATT's #1 ranking in China). Internal usage estimated at ≥95% of total Volcengine throughput.
vendors.source: Volcengine officially announced 120T/day at Wuhan AI Innovation Expo (Apr 1, 2026). IDC May 7 report independently confirms Volcengine #1 in China external MaaS at 49.5% (2025 full year). 33-day extrapolation at 8%/mo from Apr 1 anchor.

revision_history.reason: Initial estimate from Volcano Engine Q3 2025 disclosure
OFFICIAL: Volcengine 120T/day at Wuhan AI Innovation Expo Apr 1, 2026

Held. IDC May 7 report independently confirms Volcengine #1 in China at 49.5% external MaaS market share. Math check (IDC 2025 1944T / 365 × 49.5% = 2.64T/day external) vs Volcengine official 120T/day demonstrates >95% of Doubao throughput is internal first-party usage. Confidence reaffirmed at high.

vendors.note: GATT scope: all-sources. IDC scope: external MaaS only. Both rank Volcengine/Doubao #1 in China.

vendors.name: Google Gemini (All Surfaces)
vendors.source: MAJOR UPGRADE: Pichai at Cloud Next 2026 / Q1 2026 earnings (April) — 'Gemini models now process more than 16 billion tokens per minute via direct API' = 23T/day API only. Up from 10B/min in Q4 2025 (+60%). 750M Gemini App MAU. Google Cloud +48% YoY. Gemma 4 launched Apr 1. 330 organizations process >1T tokens each annually on Google Cloud.
vendors.note: Q1 2026 disclosure was the biggest single Gemini upgrade since GATT launch: API throughput jumped from 10B → 16B tokens/min in one quarter. All Surfaces estimate = API (23T) × ~3.2 multiplier for Search AI Overviews + Gemini App + Workspace = 73T (multiplier reduced from 4× since API outpaced consumer surface attach). Confidence upgraded medium-high → high.

revision_history.reason: MAJOR UPGRADE: Pichai disclosed 16B tokens/min via direct API at Cloud Next 2026 (April), up from 10B in Q4 2025 — +60% in one quarter. 16B × 60 × 24 = 23T/day API only. All Surfaces revised to 73T using 3.2× multiplier (down from 4×). Confidence upgraded medium-high → high.

vendors.name: OpenAI (ChatGPT + API)
vendors.source: OFFICIAL: 15B tokens/min API (Mar 31 — held as anchor). GPT-5.5 launched April 23 (now ChatGPT default via 5.5 Instant May 5). Some developers reportedly hitting 40M tokens/min during launch. $25B+ ARR; targeting $29.4B for 2026 (+18%). 1M+ business customers, 9M+ paying business users, 4M+ developers.
vendors.note: GPT-5.5 (Apr 23) and 5.5 Instant (May 5) launched within v0.79→v0.80 window. Token-efficient per task but spike in launch usage drives net total up. Conservative bump 44 → 45T (vs theoretical 47T at 15%/mo).

revision_history.reason: GPT-5.5 Instant May 5 became ChatGPT default. Some devs hitting 40M tokens/min during launch. 5-day extrapolation + launch boost.

vendors.name: Claude (Anthropic)
vendors.source: MAJOR: Claude MAU surged 44% Mar→Apr to 23M (BigGo/Similarweb — biggest growth among top AI apps in April). Opus 4.7 launched April 16 — new tokenizer produces up to 35% more tokens for same input. Claude Code Pro test removal Apr 21. $25B+ ARR per SemiAnalysis.
vendors.note: Three compounding tailwinds: (1) Claude MAU +44% Mar→Apr — biggest growth among top AI apps in April; (2) Opus 4.7 tokenizer change inflates token counts up to 35% for identical inputs; (3) Compute strain continues. v0.79 was 19T at 18%/mo extrapolation; v0.80 bumps to 22T to incorporate the previously-uncaptured Opus 4.7 launch and MAU surge.

revision_history.reason: MAJOR: Three previously-uncaptured signals — (1) Opus 4.7 launched April 16 with new tokenizer producing up to 35% more tokens for same input; (2) Claude MAU +44% Mar→Apr to 23M, biggest growth among top AI apps in April (BigGo/Similarweb); (3) Continued compute strain. Confidence upgraded medium-high → high.

vendors.name: DeepSeek
vendors.source: DeepSeek V4 Pro / V4 Flash launched April 24 — 1M token context, 1.6T params MoE / 49B active (V4 Pro), 284B / 13B (V4 Flash). #3 enterprise market share (behind Anthropic, OpenAI). 130M MAU. Pricing $0.145/M input, $1.74/M output (~7× cheaper than GPT-5.5).
vendors.note: V4 launch (Apr 24) is significant: 1M context window doubles V3, MoE efficiency improvements likely accelerate adoption. #3 in enterprise dev SDK usage. v0.79 had 8.2T held; v0.80 bumps to 9.2T to incorporate V4 Pro/Flash launch.

revision_history.reason: 130M MAU, V4 Lite on Huawei chips
DeepSeek V4 Pro / V4 Flash launched April 24 with 1M-token context window. Now #3 in enterprise developer SDK usage. 15-day extrapolation at 10%/mo + V4 launch boost.

vendors.name: Azure OpenAI Service + Microsoft 365 Copilot
vendors.source: Q3 FY2026 earnings (Apr 29, 2026): AI business ARR $37 billion, +123% YoY. Microsoft 365 Copilot crossed 20M paid seats (vs 15M in Jan). Azure +40% growth. GitHub Copilot 4.7M+ paid subs. Microsoft-OpenAI partnership restructured Apr 27 to non-exclusive license through 2032 (no more revenue share to OpenAI). Capex $190B for FY26.
vendors.note: Confidence upgraded low → medium. AI business ARR scale ($37B) is now disclosed quarterly — strongest enterprise AI signal in the index. Copilot seat count nearly tripled YoY. New non-exclusive structure with OpenAI may shift Microsoft toward more own-model inference.

revision_history.reason: Q3 FY2026 earnings (Apr 29): AI business ARR $37B (+123% YoY); Microsoft 365 Copilot 20M paid seats (vs 15M Jan, +33% in Q3 alone); Azure +40%. Microsoft-OpenAI partnership restructured Apr 27 (non-exclusive). Confidence upgraded low → medium.

vendors.name: Qwen (Alibaba Cloud)

Alibaba reorganized into 'Alibaba Token Hub' — consolidating Qwen, Tongyi Lab, and Wukong enterprise unit under CEO Eddie Wu. 13.9% China market share. IDC May 7 report places Alibaba Bailian #2 in China external MaaS at 27% (H1 2025).
vendors.note: Alibaba Token Hub reorg (Apr 2026) consolidating five separately operating units suggests serious enterprise AI commitment. Qwen 3.6-Plus coding upgrade aligns with developer market push.

ARR doubled in one month: $100M (Mar) → $200M (Apr). $2B funding round closing May 6, $20B post-money valuation. Weekly OpenRouter volume 1.85T tokens (#2 globally on OR).

vendors.name: OpenRouter (aggregator)
vendors.scope_note: AGGREGATOR — overlaps with vendor totals. OpenRouter routes traffic to Kimi, DeepSeek, Anthropic, OpenAI, Tencent Hy3, Xiaomi MiMo, etc. Tokens flowing through OR are also counted in those vendors' rows. Reported here as platform-scale signal, not additive to global total. The global_total_T of 310 already nets out this overlap (OR's incremental contribution is the small portion of routing not attributed to identified top-20 vendors).
vendors.source: April 2026: 20 trillion tokens/week = 2.86T/day on OpenRouter (4× YoY from 5T/week April 2025). Validated by a16z 'State of AI: An Empirical 100 Trillion Token Study' (arXiv 2601.10088, Jan 15, 2026). OpenRouter passed 1T tokens/day in December 2025. Top traffic May 2026: Kimi K2.6 (1.85T weekly), DeepSeek V4 Pro/Flash, Claude Sonnet 4, Tencent Hy3 preview, Xiaomi MiMo-V2-Pro.
vendors.note: v0.81 MAJOR REVISION: Bumped 1.27T → 3.0T/day based on OpenRouter's April 2026 20T/week disclosure and a16z 100T Token Study. Previous estimate was based on stale Feb 2026 data and missed the Mar-Apr 2026 4× YoY growth.

revision_history.reason: MAJOR REVISION: OpenRouter April 2026 disclosed 20T tokens/week = 2.86T/day (4× YoY). a16z 'State of AI' 100T Token Study (arXiv Jan 2026) confirms platform passed 1T/day in Dec 2025. Previous GATT estimate was based on stale Feb 2026 data point. Confidence remains high — OR publishes platform stats monthly. Aggregator overlap with vendor totals flagged in scope_note.
